sections.citation: cbd62c94-f560-41a5-826f-fab4c8fc1296, Page Location Text in previous version  Text in amended version, p. 1
sections.text: 5 Section 7.5 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers  Information specified in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container, and in the absence of such a container, on the product itself. However, lot identification and the name and address may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

However, lot identification and the name and address may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. However, lot identification and the name and address may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to Danbo intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this Standard.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard applies to Danbo intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this Standard.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to Danbo intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this Standard.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Danbo is a ripened firm/semi-hard cheese in conformity with the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978). The body has a near white or ivory through to light yellow or yellow colour and a firm-textured (when pressed by thumb) texture, suitable for cutting, with few to plentiful, evenly distributed, smooth and round pea sized (or mostly up to 10 mm in diameter) gas holes, but a few openings and splits are acceptable. The shape is flat squared or parallelepiped. The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without1 hard or slightly moist smear ripened rind, which may be coated. For Danbo  ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 3 weeks at 12–20°C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzym es) may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Danbo intended for further processing need not exhibit the same extent of ripening when justif ied through technical and/or trade needs.

The shape is flat squared or parallelepiped. The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without1 hard or slightly moist smear ripened rind, which may be coated. For Danbo  ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 3 weeks at 12–20°C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzym es) may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Danbo intended for further processing need not exhibit the same extent of ripening when justif ied through technical and/or trade needs.

The body has a near white or ivory through to light yellow or yellow colour and a firm-textured (when pressed by thumb) texture, suitable for cutting, with few to plentiful, evenly distributed, smooth and round pea sized (or mostly up to 10 mm in diameter) gas holes, but a few openings and splits are acceptable. The shape is flat squared or parallelepiped. The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without1 hard or slightly moist smear ripened rind, which may be coated. For Danbo  ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 3 weeks at 12–20°C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzym es) may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. sections.title: ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
number=3
level=1

sections.title: Raw materials
sections.text: Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks .
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Raw materials
number=3.1
level=2
Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks .

page_spans.text: Cows’ milk or buffaloes’ milk, or their mixtures, and products obtained from these milks .

sections.title: Permitted ingredients
sections.text: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti-caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by Good Manufacturing Practi ce, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Only those additives classes indicated as justified in the table below may be used for the product categories specified. Anticaking agents, colours and preservatives used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 01.6.2.1 (Ripened cheese, includes rind) and only certain acidity regulators, anticaking agents and colours in Table 3 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard. Additive functional class: Justified use Cheese mass Surface/rind treatment Colours:  X(a) – Bleaching agents:  – – Acidity regulators:  X – Stabilizers:  – – Thickeners:  – – Emulsifiers:  – – Antioxidants:  – – Preservatives:  X X Foaming agents:  – – Anti–caking agents:  – X(b) (a) Only to obtain the colour characteristics, as described in Section 2.  (b) For the surface of sliced, cut, shredded or grated cheese, only.  X The use of additives belonging to the class is technologically justified.  – The use of additives belonging to the class is not technologically justified.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the CAC.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CODEX CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:
sections.retrieval_text:
LABELLING
number=7
level=1
In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CODEX CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:

page_spans.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CODEX CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name Danbo may be applied in accordance with Section 4.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), provided that the product is in conformity with this Standard. Where customary in the country of retail sale, alternative spelling may be used. The use of the name is an option that may be chosen only if the cheese complies with this standard. Where the name is not used for a cheese that complies with this standard, the naming provisions of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) apply. The designation of products in which the fat content is below or above the reference range but above the absolute minimum specified in Section 3.3 of this Standard shall be accompanied by an appropriate qualification describing the modification made or the fat content (expressed as fat in dry matter or as percentage by mass whichever is acceptable in the country of retail sale), either as part of the name or in a prominent position in the same field of vision. Suitable qualifiers are the appropriate characterizing terms specified in Section 7.2 of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) or a nutritional claim in accordance with the Guidelines for Use of Nutrition and Health Claims (CXG 23-1997)2. The designation may also be used for cut, sliced, shredded or grated products made from cheese which cheese is in conformity with this Standard.

sections.title: Country of origin
sections.text: The country of origin (which means the country of manufacture, not the country in which the name originated) shall be declared. When the product undergoes substantial transformation 3 in a second country, the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling.

When the product undergoes substantial transformation 3 in a second country, the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling.

sections.title: Declaration of milk fat content
sections.text: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale, either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.

sections.title: Date marking
sections.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information, provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: METHODS OF SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti-caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by Good Manufacturing Practi ce, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.

prefilter.reason: List of ingredients and processing aids relevant to regulation.

sentences.original: The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

classification.sentence: The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the CAC.
classification.function: Compliance Requirement
classification.pattern: [Subject] shall comply with [Referenced Standards for contaminant levels and residue limits]

requirements: Maximum Levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the CAC

classification.justification: The sentence is an obligation sentence mandating compliance with specified contaminant and residue limits referencing external standards. It does not match any known template but fits an obligation category with a compliance function. The structure is a subject plus an obligation to comply with referenced quantitative standards.

Complex compliance reference involving multiple external standards and limits.

sentences.original: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

classification.sentence: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.function: Recommendation/Requirement
classification.pattern: It is recommended that <entity> be <action> in accordance with <standards>; the <entity> should comply with <criteria> established according to <guidelines>.

components.entity_1: the product covered by the provisions of this standard
components.standards: the appropriate sections of General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004), other relevant Codex texts
components.criteria_basis: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence expresses recommended obligations with modal verbs and recommendations, linking preparation, handling, and compliance to specific standards and microbiological criteria. There is no exact match in the known templates, and the sentence contains a compound structure with multiple recommended actions tied to standards and guidance. The modality is obligation as the core function is prescribing recommended practices that imply compliance requirements.
notes: The sentence includes multiple interconnected recommendations making it complex; no exact prior template available, so a new structural pattern reflecting recommendation and compliance obligation is generated.
Use of "It is recommended" and "should" conveys a normative obligation tone rather than permission or mere suggestion.

sentences.original: The country of origin (which means the country of manufacture, not the country in which the name originated) shall be declared.

sentences.original: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale, either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.

formats: as a percentage of fat in dry matter
in grams per serving as quantified in the label

sentences.original: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information, provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.

classification.function: Granting Conditional Permission
classification.pattern: [Exception clause], [Permission granted], [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.exception_clause: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)
components.permission_granted: the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information
components.condition: provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission conditionally, with an initial exception clause. It does not fit known templates but clearly expresses conditional permission with an exception clause and condition, thus a new pattern is created reflecting this structure.
notes: The sentence includes a legal exception phrase and a conditional restriction, making its structure more complex than standard templates.

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.
classification.function: Prescriptive requirement
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase indicating purpose + main clause prescribing required methods and reference standards with modal verb 'shall'

components.purpose: For checking the compliance with this standard
components.required_items: methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard

classification.justification: The sentence prescribes an obligation to use specified methods for compliance checking with normative modality 'shall'. The sentence structure includes an introductory purpose phrase followed by a prescriptive main clause. No known templates match closely, so a new pattern reflecting this structure is created.
notes: The sentence combines normative obligation with purpose indication in a complex sentence; no direct template found in catalog.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR DANBO
pages.summary: Página contém o título principal do documento e cabeçalho. Identificadas duas seções principais: '2022 AMENDMENT' e 'Page Location Text in previous version  Text in amended version'. Ambas possuem texto interno relevante e não apresentam continuidade entre páginas.

Página contém o cabeçalho 'CXS 264-1966   2' e seis nós estruturais principais incluindo três seções de nível 1 com subseções para a seção 3. O texto está corretamente associado aos respectivos títulos. A seção 3 e sua subseção 3.2 indicam continuidade para a próxima página.

A página contém as seções 3.3 Composition e 4 FOOD ADDITIVES com seus respectivos textos associados. A seção 3.3 indica continuidade da página anterior. Ambas as seções aparecem visualmente nesta página e não indicam continuação para a próxima página.

A página contém as seções 5 CONTAMINANTS, 6 HYGIENE e 7 LABELLING, sendo esta última com quatro subseções numeradas de 7.1 a 7.4. O texto de cada seção e subseção está claramente associado ao seu título. A seção 7 LABELLING indica que continua na próxima página, pois o texto ao final sugere mais conteúdo e referência a notas. O cabeçalho da página é CXS 264-1966   4, sem título principal do documento detectado nesta página.

A página contém a continuação da seção 7 LABELLING com a subseção 7.5 Labelling of non-retail containers e a nova seção 8 METHODS OF SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS. A subseção 7.5 é continuação da seção iniciada anteriormente, indicada pelo is_continuation_from_previous_page=true. Os textos estão claramente associados aos seus títulos respectivos.
